message:
type: SURVEY
files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: Implementing fallback_searchpath feature: will modify FileSystemLoader.__init__, FileSystemLoader.get_source, and FileSystemLoader.list_templates to add optional fallback_searchpath parameter/search behavior. What construct(s) in this file does your feature touch?

type: SURVEY
files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: Adding optional path_transform param to __init__ of BaseLoader-derived classes (FileSystemLoader, DictLoader, FunctionLoader, PackageLoader, PrefixLoader, ChoiceLoader) and applying it at the start of get_source (or get_loader for PrefixLoader) to transform the template path before resolution. Constructs touched: __init__ and get_source of each of those 6 classes.

type: DRAFT
files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
region: FileSystemLoader.__init__
code: def __init__(
    self,
    searchpath: t.Union[str, os.PathLike, t.Sequence[t.Union[str, os.PathLike]]],
    encoding: str = "utf-8",
    followlinks: bool = False,
    fallback_searchpath: t.Optional[
        t.Union[str, os.PathLike, t.Sequence[t.Union[str, os.PathLike]]]
    ] = None,
    path_transform: t.Optional[t.Callable[[str], str]] = None,
) -> None:
    if not isinstance(searchpath, abc.Iterable) or isinstance(searchpath, str):
        searchpath = [searchpath]

    self.searchpath = [os.fspath(p) for p in searchpath]

    if fallback_searchpath is None:
        fallback_searchpath = []
    elif not isinstance(fallback_searchpath, abc.Iterable) or isinstance(
        fallback_searchpath, str
    ):
        fallback_searchpath = [fallback_searchpath]

    self.fallback_searchpath = [os.fspath(p) for p in fallback_searchpath]

    self.encoding = encoding
    self.followlinks = followlinks
    self.path_transform = path_transform
note: Merged __init__: keeps searchpath/encoding/followlinks, adds my fallback_searchpath (defaults to empty list) and your path_transform (defaults to None). Let me know if path_transform name/type/default differs from your plan.

type: SURVEY
files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: Implementing path_transform feature: optional path_transform callable param added to __init__ of FileSystemLoader, DictLoader, FunctionLoader, PackageLoader, PrefixLoader, ChoiceLoader; applied at top of get_source (get_loader for PrefixLoader) to transform template name before resolution. Overlap with your feature: FileSystemLoader.__init__ and FileSystemLoader.get_source (I don't touch list_templates). Let's co-author those two.

type: DRAFT
files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
region: FileSystemLoader.get_source
code: def get_source(
    self, environment: "Environment", template: str
) -> t.Tuple[str, str, t.Callable[[], bool]]:
    if self.path_transform:
        template = self.path_transform(template)

    pieces = split_template_path(template)

    for searchpath in chain(self.searchpath, self.fallback_searchpath):
        filename = os.path.join(searchpath, *pieces)
        f = open_if_exists(filename)
        if f is None:
            continue
        try:
            contents = f.read().decode(self.encoding)
        finally:
            f.close()

        mtime = os.path.getmtime(filename)

        def uptodate() -> bool:
            try:
                return os.path.getmtime(filename) == mtime
            except OSError:
                return False

        return contents, filename, uptodate
    raise TemplateNotFound(template)
note: Merged get_source: applies path_transform first (your feature), then searches primary searchpath followed by fallback_searchpath in order (my feature). Uses itertools.chain - will add 'from itertools import chain' import. Confirm this matches your path_transform usage.

type: AGREE

region: FileSystemLoader.__init__
code: def __init__(
    self,
    searchpath: t.Union[str, os.PathLike, t.Sequence[t.Union[str, os.PathLike]]],
    encoding: str = "utf-8",
    followlinks: bool = False,
    fallback_searchpath: t.Optional[
        t.Union[str, os.PathLike, t.Sequence[t.Union[str, os.PathLike]]]
    ] = None,
    path_transform: t.Optional[t.Callable[[str], str]] = None,
) -> None:
    if not isinstance(searchpath, abc.Iterable) or isinstance(searchpath, str):
        searchpath = [searchpath]

    self.searchpath = [os.fspath(p) for p in searchpath]

    if fallback_searchpath is None:
        fallback_searchpath = []
    elif not isinstance(fallback_searchpath, abc.Iterable) or isinstance(
        fallback_searchpath, str
    ):
        fallback_searchpath = [fallback_searchpath]

    self.fallback_searchpath = [os.fspath(p) for p in fallback_searchpath]

    self.encoding = encoding
    self.followlinks = followlinks
    self.path_transform = path_transform

type: DRAFT
files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
region: FileSystemLoader.get_source
code: def get_source(
    self, environment: "Environment", template: str
) -> t.Tuple[str, str, t.Callable[[], bool]]:
    if self.path_transform is not None:
        template = self.path_transform(template)

    pieces = split_template_path(template)
    for searchpath in self.searchpath + self.fallback_searchpath:
        filename = os.path.join(searchpath, *pieces)
        f = open_if_exists(filename)
        if f is None:
            continue
        try:
            contents = f.read().decode(self.encoding)
        finally:
            f.close()

        mtime = os.path.getmtime(filename)

        def uptodate() -> bool:
            try:
                return os.path.getmtime(filename) == mtime
            except OSError:
                return False

        return contents, filename, uptodate
    raise TemplateNotFound(template)
note: Applies path_transform first, then searches primary searchpath followed by fallback_searchpath. If your fallback logic differs (e.g. only fallback after primary fully exhausted vs interleaved), let me know — this does primary+fallback as one combined list which is equivalent to sequential search since we return on first match.
